title: Middle Eastern Citrus and Goat Cheese Bake
description: A flavorful and refreshing Middle Eastern-inspired dish featuring citrusy flavors, crispy texture, and a tangy goat cheese topping. Perfect for a light lunch or dinner.
instructions: Cut the fennel into bite-sized pieces and place on a baking sheet.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt.
Bake for 15-20 minutes or until tender and slightly caramelized.
Meanwhile, slice the oranges into thin rounds and place on a separate baking sheet.
Bake for 10-12 minutes or until slightly caramelized and tender.
In a separate pan, gently heat the goat cheese over low heat until softened.
To assemble the dish, place a slice of pita bread on a plate, top with a spoonful of the goat cheese mixture, followed by a piece of the baked fennel, an orange slice, and a sprinkle of fresh mint.
Repeat the layers one more time, finishing with a slice of orange on top.
Bake for an additional 5-7 minutes or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
